name: Doughnuts with Kaluha Custard
recipeInstructions:
Doughnuts: In a coffee mug dissolve sugar and ground ginger in warm water.
Stir in the yeast and let stand for 10-15 minutes. Scald milk in a saucepan and pour into a large mixing bowl. Add sugar, salt, and shortening. Stir in egg and about 1 cup flour. By this time the yeast in the mug should have a good foamy head. Stir it into the flour, egg mixture. Add another cup of flour and beat well by hand until smooth. Gradually beat in the remaining flour. Turn out onto a floured board and knead by hand for 10 minutes. The dough should be very smooth, satiny, and light. Place the dough in a clean buttered bowl, brush the top with a little butter, and cover with a warm damp cloth. Place in a warm place and let stand about 1 hour until it has doubled in size. Punch down the dough and roll it onto a floured board to 1/2-inch thickness. Cut into squares or rectangles measuring 2 inches by 3 inches using a knife or pastry cutter. Cover and let sit until doubled in size, about 45 minutes. Heat clean vegetable oil in a deep fryer or large skillet to 375 degrees Fahrenheit. Using a wide spatula, slide the doughnuts into the oil and fry until golden, turning once. Lift out and drain on paper towels. When they are cool enough to handle, take each one in turn and using a large kitchen syringe inject each doughnut with about 1 ounce of custard. Dip doughnuts into sugar glaze and let cool on a wire rack. These must be eaten fresh.
